scope.summary:
Goal: Make HANDSHAKE LAUNCH palmistry.exe at startup and complete the startup IPC HANDSHAKE (Master Spec v02.196 §6.13.3 'launched WITH Handshake at startup' + §6.13.2). At Handshake startup, the native app spawns the sibling `palmistry` process, passes it the Handshake PID + session-id + ring backing-file path + control-socket name (the MT-089 inputs), and completes a startup handshake (Palmistry acks it is watching). This MUST honor HBR-QUIET (§6.13 cross-ref HBR-QUIET-003): the spawned Palmistry process must NOT steal foreground / pop a window / hijack input — it is a headless background watcher.

LIFECYCLE-INVERSION LAUNCH CONSTRAINT (the HARD §6.13.3 contract on the launcher, from MT-089): the spawn MUST NOT add Palmistry to a Win32 Job Object with KILL_ON_JOB_CLOSE (and must not put it in a process group that dies with the parent). A normal `std::process::Command::spawn` on Windows does NOT add the child to the parent's job by default, BUT eframe/winit or a wrapping launcher might; the spawn must explicitly ensure Palmistry is a free-standing process that survives parent death (MT-089 AC-009-4 is the behavioral proof; this MT must spawn it in a way that preserves that). Document the spawn flags used (e.g. on Windows, DETACHED_PROCESS / CREATE_NO_WINDOW for quiet headless, and NOT adding it to a kill-on-close job).

Exact files to create/edit:
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/diagnostics/palmistry_launch.rs` — `pub fn launch_palmistry(session: &DiagSession, ring_path: &Path, control_socket: &str) -> io::Result<PalmistryHandle>`. It resolves the palmistry.exe path RELATIVE to the running Handshake exe (`std::env::current_exe()` -> sibling `palmistry.exe`) — portable, no hardcoded path (the installer ships both binaries side by side; install_payload in handshake-native Cargo.toml already lists a two-binary layout — add palmistry.exe to that payload). Spawns it via `std::process::Command` with: the PID/session/ring/socket passed as env vars or args (matching MT-089's cli.rs intake), CREATE_NO_WINDOW + a detached/quiet flag (HBR-QUIET — no console window, no focus steal), stdout/stderr piped or to a log file (not inherited to steal the console). Returns a handle holding the child + the control-socket client for sending Shutdown later.
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/main.rs` (or `app.rs` HandshakeApp::new) — call `launch_palmistry(...)` AFTER the ring writer is created (MT-082 install) + the session id is minted, EARLY in startup so the watcher is up before the app can freeze. Store the PalmistryHandle on HandshakeApp so a clean app shutdown sends the Shutdown control message (the §6.13.3 'closes only on explicit command' path — on a normal Handshake exit, send Shutdown so Palmistry exits cleanly + records NO crash). Graceful degradation: if palmistry.exe is missing or the spawn fails, log a tracing::warn + a typed BackendUnreachable-style diagnostic and CONTINUE (Handshake must run even without the watcher — the watcher is supplementary; never block startup on it).
- The IPC HANDSHAKE: after spawn, Handshake connects to Palmistry's control socket (MT-089 control.rs) and exchanges a `HandshakeHello{ pid, session_id, ring_path }` / ack so both sides confirm the channel + the ring binding before the app proceeds. Bounded: if Palmistry does not ack within a short deadline, log + continue degraded (do not hang startup waiting on the watcher).
- On clean Handshake shutdown (eframe on_exit / Drop): send the Shutdown control message so Palmistry exits cleanly (the clean-shutdown-is-not-a-crash path from MT-089/012).
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_palmistry_launch.rs`.

THE LAUNCH PROOF (the deliverable, §6.13.3): a test launches the real HandshakeApp startup path (or directly calls launch_palmistry against the built palmistry.exe) and asserts: (1) palmistry.exe is SPAWNED (a child process exists), (2) the startup HANDSHAKE completes (Palmistry acks over the control socket), (3) HBR-QUIET — the spawn used CREATE_NO_WINDOW / detached + no focus steal (verified via the spawn flags + the existing focus-audit approach — no SetForegroundWindow), (4) on a clean shutdown, Handshake sends Shutdown and Palmistry exits cleanly (no crash record), (5) graceful degradation — with palmistry.exe absent, Handshake still starts (the spawn failure is logged + degraded, startup not blocked).

Reuse note: the palmistry.exe path resolves via std::env::current_exe() (portable; the same approach installer self-check uses for bundled assets). HBR-QUIET / focus-safety is the EXISTING handshake-native discipline (quiet_mode/focus_guard.rs + the test_focus_audit_quiet.rs source scan + the CREATE_NO_WINDOW pattern already used for the MT-088 LSP subprocess spawn in this crate — reuse that exact spawn discipline). DiagSession (session id + ring path) is from MT-082. The control socket + HandshakeHello are MT-089. src/backend untouched.

Risk if missed: Palmistry is never launched (the whole Tier 3 watcher is dormant — built but never started); OR a non-quiet spawn pops a console/steals focus (HBR-QUIET violation during model testing); OR the spawn adds Palmistry to a kill-on-close job, breaking the survives-parent-death inversion; OR a blocking handshake hangs Handshake startup waiting on the watcher (reintroducing a startup stall).
forbidden_paths: src/frontend/palmistry/** (the watcher is MT-089..013; this MT only LAUNCHES it + handshakes)
acceptance_criteria: AC-014-1 (LAUNCH + HANDSHAKE via the FUNCTION, not the windowed app): a BOUNDED test calls launch_palmistry() directly with test inputs and asserts (a) a real palmistry.exe child process spawns, (b) the startup IPC handshake acks over the MT-089 control socket with the matching pid/session/ring, (c) the child is then reaped via Shutdown. The main() WIRING (main() calls launch_palmistry before eframe::run_native) is proven by a source-scan assertion on main.rs. The real windowed binary is NEVER executed-and-waited-on in a test (eframe::run_native never returns -> hang).

AC-014-2 (HBR-QUIET): the spawn is headless + quiet — CREATE_NO_WINDOW / detached, stdout/stderr not inherited to a console, NO SetForegroundWindow / focus steal — verified via the spawn flags + the existing focus-audit/quiet-mode source discipline (no foreground call), consistent with the crate's existing subprocess-spawn pattern (the MT-088 LSP CREATE_NO_WINDOW spawn).

AC-014-3 (NOT kill-on-job-close): the spawn does NOT add Palmistry to a kill-on-job-close Win32 Job Object / parent-dies process group — documented spawn flags + the MT-089 survives-parent-death behavior preserved (the launcher-side half of the §6.13.3 inversion contract).

AC-014-4 (CLEAN SHUTDOWN): on a clean Handshake exit, Handshake sends the Shutdown control message and Palmistry exits cleanly with NO crash record — proven by a test that runs the startup + clean-shutdown path and asserts Palmistry got Shutdown + exited + wrote no crash artifact.

AC-014-5 (GRACEFUL DEGRADATION): with palmistry.exe absent (or the spawn forced to fail), Handshake STILL starts (the failure is logged + recorded as a typed diagnostic; startup is NOT blocked and does NOT hang on the watcher handshake) — proven by a test removing/mis-pointing the exe and asserting the app starts.

AC-014-6: palmistry.exe is added to the install two-binary payload (install_payload in handshake-native Cargo.toml metadata) so the installer ships it side-by-side; the exe path resolves via current_exe() (portable, no hardcoded path).
AC-014-7: `cargo build -p handshake-native` (lib + bins) clean; clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` clean; full suite `-j 2` green; external target dir; no repo-local target/, no tracked artifact.
proof_targets: PT-014-A: launch test GREEN — palmistry.exe spawned + startup handshake acked.
PT-014-B: HBR-QUIET spawn-flags + focus-audit assertions GREEN (no window, no focus steal).
PT-014-C: clean-shutdown sends Shutdown + Palmistry exits with no crash; graceful-degradation (exe absent) startup-still-runs assertions GREEN.
PT-014-D: install_payload includes palmistry.exe; current_exe()-relative resolution; `cargo build --lib --bins` clean + clippy clean + full suite -j 2 green.
implementation_notes: CRITICAL TEST STEER (the MT-094 HANG 2026-06-28: a test launched the REAL windowed handshake-native binary and WAITED on it; eframe::run_native+'+s event loop NEVER EXITS -> the app spun at 100% CPU -> cargo/agent blocked 1h10m). HARD RULES, no exceptions: (1) Put launch_palmistry() in main() startup BEFORE eframe::run_native - NOT in HandshakeApp::new - so that kittest build_eframe tests ACROSS THE WHOLE SUITE (MT-082/084/087 etc. all call HandshakeApp::new) do NOT each spawn + leak a palmistry child. (2) The launch PROOF (AC-014-1) tests launch_palmistry() DIRECTLY: call the function with test inputs -> assert a real palmistry.exe child spawns + the startup handshake acks over the MT-089 control socket -> send Shutdown -> assert clean exit -> REAP the child. EVERYTHING BOUNDED (wait_for_exit/connect/read with explicit timeouts; NO unbounded Command::output()/read_line). (3) ABSOLUTELY NEVER spawn the real handshake-native windowed binary (Command::new(<handshake_native exe>).spawn) and wait on it, and NEVER call eframe::run_native in a test - the windowed event loop does not return and WILL hang. Prove the main() WIRING (that main() calls launch_palmistry before run_native) with a SOURCE-SCAN assertion on main.rs, NOT by executing the windowed app. #[ignore]-with-reason any test that genuinely needs the real windowed app (run --ignored on a real host, closed by a human). (4) Every spawned palmistry MUST be reaped (send Shutdown, then kill-on-timeout) so it neither leaks (no orphan ping/palmistry) nor records a crash for the test-process death.

BOUNDED-TEST RULE (MT-092 precedent, MANDATORY): any test spawning the palmistry binary / doing minidumper-IPC / waiting on a child MUST use an explicit timeout (wait_for_exit/connect/read) - NEVER an unbounded Command::output() or socket read_line. #[ignore] with a typed reason any test that deadlocks under this headless harness (run with --ignored on a real host); minidumper out-of-process IPC + simulate_exception are the known hangers. Run palmistry tests under a hard external bound so a deadlock cannot waste hours.

Resolve palmistry.exe via std::env::current_exe() -> its sibling directory (the installer ships both side-by-side; the self-check pattern already resolves bundled assets relative to the exe). Portable, no hardcoded path.

Spawn with CREATE_NO_WINDOW + a detached/quiet disposition (HBR-QUIET) — reuse the EXACT subprocess-spawn discipline the crate already uses for the MT-088 LSP server (CREATE_NO_WINDOW, focus-safe). Do NOT inherit the console; pipe or log stdout/stderr.

Do NOT add Palmistry to a kill-on-job-close Job Object (§6.13.3). A plain Command::spawn does not by default, but VERIFY nothing in the launch wrapper does; the MT-089 survives-parent-death test is the behavioral proof this launcher must preserve.

The startup handshake must be BOUNDED — if Palmistry does not ack within a short deadline, log + continue degraded. NEVER hang Handshake startup waiting on the watcher (that would reintroduce a startup stall, the opposite of the WP's purpose).
Store the PalmistryHandle on HandshakeApp; on clean exit (eframe on_exit/Drop) send Shutdown so Palmistry closes cleanly + records NO crash (the §6.13 clean-shutdown rule).
GRACEFUL DEGRADATION: the watcher is SUPPLEMENTARY (§5.8.6) — if it cannot be launched, Handshake runs anyway. Log + a typed diagnostic; never block startup.

Add palmistry.exe to the install_payload metadata (handshake-native Cargo.toml [workspace.metadata.handshake-native] install_payload already lists handshake-native.exe + handshake_core.exe — add palmistry.exe).
Read the KERNEL_BUILDER coder protocol under .GOV/ first; the launch must spawn the REAL built palmistry.exe + complete a REAL handshake (Spec-Realism), not a mocked spawn.
binds_backend_api: NONE. Handshake spawns the sibling palmistry.exe + handshakes over the local control socket. No backend involvement.

hbr_int_009_tier_obligations.reason: Launch + handshake do not touch the Tier 1 FR; FR forwarding is MT-093.

The launch passes the MT-082 DiagSession (session id + ring path) so Palmistry binds to the Tier 2 internal_diagnostics ring; a failed launch is recorded as a typed internal_diagnostics event (graceful degradation). The Tier 2 -> Tier 3 binding is established here.

This MT IS the §6.13.3 'launched WITH Handshake' half: Handshake spawns palmistry.exe quietly (HBR-QUIET), completes the startup handshake, preserves the not-kill-on-job-close inversion, and sends Shutdown on clean exit. Built + proven here.

risks: RISK-014-1: Palmistry is never launched (built but dormant) — the whole Tier 3 is dead. Mitigate: the launch is wired into startup + AC-014-1 proves a real spawn + handshake.

RISK-014-2: a non-quiet spawn pops a console / steals focus (HBR-QUIET violation during model testing). Mitigate: CREATE_NO_WINDOW + detached + no foreground call (AC-014-2), reusing the crate's existing focus-safe spawn discipline.
RISK-014-3: the spawn adds Palmistry to a kill-on-close job, breaking survives-parent-death. Mitigate: documented spawn flags + MT-089 behavioral proof preserved (AC-014-3).
RISK-014-4: a blocking handshake hangs Handshake startup waiting on the watcher (reintroduces a startup stall). Mitigate: bounded handshake + continue degraded (AC-014-5).
RISK-014-5: a missing palmistry.exe crashes/blocks startup instead of degrading. Mitigate: graceful degradation, startup still runs (AC-014-5).
RISK-014-6: a hardcoded exe path breaks portability / the installer. Mitigate: current_exe()-relative resolution + install_payload entry (AC-014-6).
minimum_controls: MC-014-1: real spawn + real startup handshake (AC-014-1).
MC-014-2: HBR-QUIET headless spawn, no focus steal (AC-014-2).
MC-014-4: bounded handshake + graceful degradation, startup never blocked (AC-014-5).

research_provenance.source: WP-KERNEL-012 field research (June 2026); reuses the crate's MT-088 CREATE_NO_WINDOW subprocess-spawn discipline
research_provenance.field_stack: current_exe()-relative sibling-binary resolution (portable, installer-friendly); CREATE_NO_WINDOW/detached quiet spawn (the crate's existing focus-safe subprocess pattern); a bounded startup IPC handshake over the interprocess local socket; Shutdown-on-clean-exit so the watcher closes cleanly (Crashpad's handler-launched-by-client model).

crate_versions_verified_2026_06_27.windows-sys: 0.61 (CREATE_NO_WINDOW / DETACHED_PROCESS creation flags; already the crate's Windows family)

research_provenance.motivating_incident: The 2026-06-26 freeze is only observable by an external watcher if that watcher is actually RUNNING when the freeze happens — so Handshake must launch Palmistry at startup, quietly, before the app can stall, without blocking startup on it.
research_provenance.validation_plan: real spawn + handshake + HBR-QUIET flags + not-kill-on-job-close + clean-shutdown + graceful-degradation + install_payload; clippy -D warnings; full suite -j 2.

implementation_result.summary: Handshake launches Palmistry at startup: launch_palmistry_or_degrade() spawns the real palmistry.exe (current_exe()-sibling, install_payload) BEFORE eframe::run_native, completes a BOUNDED HandshakeHello/Ack over the MT-089 interprocess control socket (recv_timeout 4s, degrades to None if absent), stores the handle on HandshakeApp, sends Shutdown on on_exit (clean exit, no crash record, CleanShutdown survivor). HBR-QUIET (CREATE_NO_WINDOW + null stdio + no foreground; focus-audit 7/7). NOT kill-on-job-close (plain std Command, no Job Object; source-scan asserts absence). Graceful degradation (<2s, startup not blocked). PROVEN by a live BOUNDED direct test (launch_palmistry_at with test inputs) + main() SOURCE-SCAN — the windowed binary is NEVER executed (the anti-hang fix). AC-014-1..6 PASS; AC-014-7 PARTIAL (full -j2 suite deferred to WP validation per the 2026-06-28 lightened pipeline). +1142/-3, 7 files.
implementation_result.gate: own-tests-green (5/5 launch + 6/6 unit + focus/heartbeat/diag-panel no-regression) per diagnostic_gate_policy
